Meta.Description: Immutable Source of Truth for Backend API and Database Schema
Meta.Usage: Paste this into the AI Context before generating any API or Data Fetching code.

Protocol.Rule: Single Entry Point (one POST endpoint). RESTful routes (GET /transactions) DO NOT exist.

Authorization: Bearer <JWT>. Each request carries a Supabase session JWT in the Authorization header. The legacy body "key" field is REMOVED. Unauthenticated/invalid-token requests return { status: 'error' }.

family_id is SERVER-DERIVED from the authenticated session and applied server-side. A client-supplied family_id/user_id in the body is ignored/overwritten. Row-Level Security enforces per-family isolation as the backstop.
Client must use a Request Queue. Network calls are strictly serial.
Strict. Unknown keys in payload will trigger a generic API error.

Action.name: read
Action.Description: Delta Sync. Returns rows modified since timestamp.

Action.name: batchCreate
Action.Description: Bulk Insert. ID is optional (generated by server if missing).

Action.name: batchUpdate
Action.Description: Bulk Edit. Updates ONLY the specific keys provided.

Action.name: batchUpsert
Action.Description: Idempotent Save. Creates if ID missing, Updates if ID exists. Best for Seeding Test Data and sync creates.

Action.name: wipe
Action.Description: Destructive (Test Mode Only). Clears all data in the specified table for the caller's family.

DatabaseSchema.Note: These are LOGICAL KEYS (snake_case). Do not use physical Table Headers.

Column.description: Tenancy key. Derived from the session; NEVER accepted from the client body. Present on all domain tables.
Primary Key as UUID, or special cases for 'immutable / test' logic
Uppercase only, whitespace permisable, JSON string, e.g. ["TAG 1", "TAG @2"]
Primary Key, formatted as 'c001', or special cases for 'immutable' logic
Primary Key, formatted as 'sc001', or special cases for 'immutable' logic
FK -> Categories. Tenancy via the parent category.
True for the permanent 'General' default of a user category (not renamable/deletable).
Primary Key, formatted as 'w001', or special cases for 'immutable' logic
Primary Key, formatted as 'e001', or special cases for 'immutable' logic
Primary Key, formatted as 'm001', or special cases for 'immutable' logic
Auth-user link. SERVER-DERIVED, read-only: present in reads, ignored/stripped on writes. The client derives 'Me' as the member whose user_id equals the session uid.

Google sign-in photo. SERVER-MANAGED, read-only: written by the sign-in trigger (handle_new_user), present in reads, ignored/stripped on writes. Render precedence is custom avatar -> google_avatar -> generated initials, so it is a default that never overwrites a custom avatar.

Table.name: STAGED_TRANSACTIONS
Table.Note: Synced import staging area. Rows are partially-mapped, possibly-invalid import candidates the client bulk-edits before promotion. On commit, valid rows are copied into TRANSACTIONS (client-orchestrated batchCreate) and the staging rows are soft-deleted. RLS-scoped by family_id like every domain table.

Column.description: Groups all rows from one import run; enables bulk clear/delete of a batch.
ISO YYYY-MM-DD when resolved; may be empty/unparsed while staged.
RAW import text (may carry a leading sign or currency symbol). Parsed to a Number on promotion to TRANSACTIONS.amount. NOT a Number here, so unparsed/invalid input can persist.
ISO code; nullable until derived from the mapped wallet.
'income' | 'expense'; nullable until the category is mapped.
JSON string array; same convention as TRANSACTIONS.tags.
'cleared' | 'pending'; the transaction status carried through to the promoted row.
Raw source-column value for wallet, kept for re-mapping in the bulk-edit table.
Raw source-column value for category, kept for re-mapping.
Raw source-column value for the entity/name, kept for re-mapping.

The full original import row as a JSON object keyed by source header. Lets the bulk-edit grid show and filter ANY source column, mapped or not (e.g. a country-code column never mapped to a target field). source_wallet/category/name remain as denormalized conveniences.
